commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `.github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml` with pull-request self-proof and manual dispatch inputs to run QA Lab `smoke-ci` or a direct scenario and upload `.artifacts/qa-e2e/`.

PR surface: Config +183. Total +183 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a new CI/admin workflow surface. The proof path is a successful GitHub runner execution that produces and uploads QA evidence artifacts.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is maintainer workflow ownership plus runner proof, not a narrow code defect for automated repair.

target.title: ci: draft QA Lab smoke profile dispatch wiring
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a dispatch-only `QA Lab Smoke Profile` GitHub workflow that runs the existing taxonomy-backed `smoke-ci` QA Lab profile on Ubuntu runners.
- Provides a direct `scenario_id` override for the follow-up UX Matrix scenario so maintainers can dispatch the same workflow against PR 3 once that scenario ID lands.
- Uploads `.artifacts/qa-e2e/` so QA Lab evidence tooling can consume `qa-evidence.json`, suite summaries, logs, JSONL, screenshots, and videos from the runner artifact.
- Intentionally avoids editing existing release/smoke workflows, adding mobile simulator/emulator lanes, or changing QA Lab runtime behavior.
- This is intentionally **draft workflow wiring** because maintainers may choose to fold this command shape into the planned smoke-ci workflow instead of landing a standalone workflow.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #94276
Related #94283
Related #94306

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Requested as a draft follow-up slice for GitHub Action / smoke-profile wiring after the QA Lab UX Matrix scenario work, with the caveat that the final smoke-ci integration may be renamed or folded into maintainer-owned smoke-ci work.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: QA Lab evidence can be dispatched on GitHub runners through a dedicated `smoke-ci` profile workflow, with an explicit `scenario_id` path reserved for the UX Matrix scenario from PR 3.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on `codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action` at `7510ca31bc`, rebased directly on current `origin/main` `9477b11d98`. This PR now changes only `.github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - `PATH="/Users/solvely/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
  - `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml`
  - `gh workflow run qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw --ref codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action -f ref=codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action -f qa_profile=smoke-ci -f concurrency=1 -f evidence_mode=slim -f fast=true`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
# no output; exited 0

$ PATH="/Users/solvely/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" node scripts/check-workflows.mjs
zizmor...................................................................Passed
No direct inputs interpolation found in composite run blocks.

$ go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml
# no output; actionlint exited 0

$ gh workflow run qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw --ref codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action -f ref=codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action -f qa_profile=smoke-ci -f concurrency=1 -f evidence_mode=slim -f fast=true
HTTP 404: workflow qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml not found on the default branch (https://api.github.com/repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml)
```

- Observed result after fix: The workflow is syntactically valid and the PR now has workflow-only scope. A direct GitHub `workflow_dispatch` cannot be run before merge because GitHub only exposes manual dispatch for workflows present on the repository default branch.
- What was not tested: No successful GitHub runner dispatch of this new workflow file yet. That needs either a maintainer-approved existing/default-branch workflow path or a post-merge dispatch.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This draft is intentionally a wiring slice. Default execution is headless/mock-provider only and avoids live services, Android emulators, and iOS simulators.
- Before evidence: Existing release QA workflows can run QA Lab lanes, but there was no small dispatch-only runner entry point for smoke profile evidence or the upcoming UX Matrix scenario override.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `PATH="/Users/solvely/.local/bin:/opt/homebrew/bin:$PATH" node scripts/check-workflows.mjs`
- `go run github.com/rhysd/actionlint/cmd/actionlint@v1.7.11 .github/workflows/qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml`
- `gh workflow run qa-lab-smoke-profile.yml --repo openclaw/openclaw --ref codex/qa-lab-smoke-profile-action ...` -> blocked by GitHub default-branch workflow-dispatch limitation above.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

No runtime tests were added; this is workflow/config wiring over existing QA Lab CLI contracts.

What failed before this fix, if known?

The missing piece was not a failing code path; it was the absence of a dispatchable runner path for smoke-profile/UX Matrix QA evidence artifacts.

If no test was added, why not?

The workflow validates through the repo workflow sanity script, actionlint, and the existing QA Lab command surfaces. A real runner dispatch requires the workflow to exist on the repository default branch or to be folded into an existing default-branch workflow.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No runtime config or migration behavior changed. This adds a manually dispatched GitHub workflow.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, narrowly: a new manually dispatched workflow can run QA Lab commands on GitHub-hosted runners with read-only contents permissions and live provider keys scrubbed.

What is the highest-risk area?

Workflow ownership/shape may overlap with maintainer-owned smoke-ci work, and direct manual dispatch cannot be proven before the workflow exists on default.

How is that risk mitigated?

The PR is draft, dispatch-only, does not rename or replace existing workflows, uses the existing `smoke-ci` QA profile by default, exposes `scenario_id` as the minimal PR 3 integration target, is now workflow-only on current `main`, and documents the exact GitHub dispatch limitation.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer decision needed: keep this as a dedicated draft workflow and accept that first successful dispatch proof is post-merge/default-branch, or fold the command shape into an existing maintainer-owned smoke-ci workflow so runner proof can happen before merge.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on maintainer direction for dedicated workflow vs smoke-ci fold-in. Also waiting on PR 3 to provide the final UX Matrix scenario ID.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Rebased directly on current `origin/main` so this PR is workflow-only and no longer carries #94283 gallery runtime/UI changes.
- Reconciled the PR description/risk section to state clearly that this is draft workflow wiring, not a runtime/UI change.
- Investigated ClawSweeper’s runner-proof request directly. The requested `workflow_dispatch` command was attempted and failed with GitHub’s default-branch workflow lookup limitation, so this remains draft and the limitation is documented instead of claiming unavailable proof.
